summary: Deliver the first M4 render-derived slice: application-owned strong geometry identity, per-document previous-success deltas, truthful unavailable and non-applicable states, and complete status-bar presentation without allowing stale, failed, or superseded work to advance or mislabel the baseline.

decisions.decision: Compute exact SHA-256 identities in the application runtime over the accepted 3D mesh byte view or exact 2D SVG UTF-8 bytes, overwrite rather than trust engine-supplied identity strings, validate the sha256:<lowercase-hex> shape wherever identities cross a worker boundary, and report comparison unavailable when application hashing cannot establish proof.
decisions.rationale: Native and WASM adapters must feed one comparison rule. Trusting arbitrary worker or adapter strings could produce a false unchanged claim, while treating missing crypto as changed would also be misleading. Application ownership gives both paths the same invariant and preserves an honest unavailable state.

decisions.decision: Compare each accepted success with the immediately previous accepted success for that document across preview and full quality; matching strong identities are unchanged, while changed comparable results retain signed volume, triangle, and bounding-box minimum, maximum, and extent deltas. Do not advance the baseline for failure, cancellation, stale completion, or superseded work, and label 2D, missing, or cross-dimension metrics without inventing values.
decisions.rationale: The viewer presentation is already the stable per-document last-success owner. It prevents tab bleed and gives failed or stale work no authority, while complete signed bounds distinguish translation from resizing and preserve the conservative Q-0035 interpretation.

decisions.topic: Complete geometry status in a fixed-height bar
decisions.decision: Keep baseline, unchanged, and comparison-unavailable labels directly visible; for changed geometry show a bounded one-line summary naming volume, bounds, and triangles, with a keyboard-operable details disclosure containing the complete signed readout. Preserve nonzero values below 0.001 through explicit threshold notation instead of rounding them to zero.
decisions.rationale: A single full metric sentence can be clipped by the fixed-height desktop and narrow status bar. A compact summary plus focusable disclosure keeps all required values discoverable without expanding the workspace or falsifying small changes.

testEvidence.criterion: The previous-success state is per document and classifies first, matching, changed, unavailable, and incomparable geometry without inventing statistics.
testEvidence.test: Drive the pure viewer reducer and geometry-delta functions through baseline, equal and unequal identities, 2D/3D transitions, missing statistics, malformed identities, and interleaved documents.

red.outcome: RED: the first accepted result had no geometryDelta and the assertion expected { kind: 'baseline' }. Later focused RED runs also proved missing signed metrics, malformed-identity trust, and unavailable-comparison defects before their fixes.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/geometry/geometry-delta.test.ts tests/application/geometry/geometry-identity.test.ts tests/application/viewer/viewer-state.test.ts tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts tests/ui/workbench-status.test.ts tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx tests/ui/viewer/active-viewer-presentation.test.ts tests/platform-web/wasm-engine-service.test.ts tests/platform-desktop/tauri-bridge.test.ts
green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 9 passed (9); Tests 94 passed (94); Duration 4.98s.

testEvidence.criterion: Cosmetic source edits report Geometry unchanged, a real geometry edit exposes a nonzero volume delta, stale claims disappear, and the complete metric set remains keyboard accessible.
testEvidence.test: Render a baseline in Workbench, edit only a comment and rerender identical bytes, edit real source and return changed bytes and statistics, then inspect the compact summary and details disclosure.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx -t "geometry baseline"
red.outcome: RED: the status footer contained only engine, render, diagnostics, cursor, encoding, and theme content; Geometry baseline established was absent. Subsequent focused RED runs exposed stale unchanged copy and the missing complete-details disclosure.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx -t "geometry baseline"
green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 1 passed and 20 skipped by the explicit name filter; Duration 2.14s.

testEvidence.criterion: The changed contracts and UI remain type-correct after the complete focused batch.
testEvidence.test: Compile the complete TypeScript application and tests without emission.

red.outcome: RED: WebCrypto digest rejected Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike> at geometry-identity.ts because SharedArrayBuffer is not a DOM BufferSource.

green.outcome: GREEN: tsc --noEmit exited 0 with no diagnostics.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
